title: Merger & Acquisition Term Sheet — Acquisition of TargetCo Inc. by AcquirerCorp

clauses.text: AcquirerCorp shall acquire substantially all of the assets of TargetCo for a total purchase price of Two Hundred Fifty Million Dollars ($250,000,000), subject to adjustments set forth herein.

TargetCo and its shareholders jointly and severally indemnify AcquirerCorp, its officers, directors, employees, agents, successors, and assigns against any and all losses, damages, costs, expenses (including attorneys' fees), and liabilities of any kind arising from or relating to the business of TargetCo prior to the Closing Date, with no cap on such indemnification obligations.
AcquirerCorp shall indemnify TargetCo shareholders for any losses arising directly from AcquirerCorp's post-closing operation of the acquired business, limited to the Purchase Price.

Any disputes arising under this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Cayman Islands, and all parties irrevocably waive any objection to such jurisdiction or any claim of inconvenient forum.
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflicts of law principles.

AcquirerCorp's total aggregate liability for any claims arising under or related to this Agreement, including indemnification obligations, representations and warranty breaches, and any other claims, shall be limited to Ten Million Dollars ($10,000,000), regardless of the nature or number of claims.

Notwithstanding any other provision herein, AcquirerCorp shall have no liability whatsoever for any pre-closing liabilities of TargetCo, including any tax obligations, pending litigation, regulatory violations, or environmental liabilities, all of which shall remain the sole responsibility of TargetCo and its shareholders.

TargetCo represents and warrants that: (i) it has full legal authority to enter into this Agreement; (ii) all financial statements provided are accurate and complete in all material respects; (iii) there are no undisclosed material liabilities; (iv) all material contracts are in full force and effect; (v) TargetCo is in compliance with all applicable laws.

TargetCo's representations and warranties shall survive the Closing for a period of eighteen (18) months, after which they shall expire and be of no further force or effect, except for representations relating to fundamental matters (organization, authority, title to assets) which shall survive indefinitely.

AcquirerCorp represents and warrants that it has conducted its own independent due diligence and is not relying on any representations or warranties not expressly set forth in this Agreement. AcquirerCorp acknowledges that it acquires the business on an 'as-is' basis with respect to all matters not expressly warranted herein.

All employees of TargetCo shall be deemed terminated as of the Closing Date. AcquirerCorp may, at its sole discretion, offer employment to some or all such employees on terms and conditions determined entirely by AcquirerCorp. AcquirerCorp shall have no obligation to retain any TargetCo employee or to assume any existing employment obligations.

The purchase price shall be subject to adjustment based on a final net working capital calculation to be completed within 90 days post-closing. Any dispute regarding the net working capital calculation shall be resolved by an independent accountant mutually agreed upon by the parties.

TargetCo shareholders agree to a non-compete covenant for a period of five (5) years in any jurisdiction worldwide in which TargetCo has conducted or plans to conduct business, covering any business activity that may in any way compete with the acquired business or any future business of AcquirerCorp.

AcquirerCorp shall assume all existing customer contracts of TargetCo. TargetCo represents that all customer contracts are assignable without consent, and that no customer has any right to terminate or renegotiate terms upon the change of control.

TargetCo indemnifies AcquirerCorp against any environmental contamination or liability at any property ever owned, operated, or leased by TargetCo, including properties divested more than ten years prior to Closing, with no time limitation on such indemnification.
The Closing shall occur on a date mutually agreed by the parties, but in no event later than 120 days from the execution date of this term sheet, unless extended by written agreement.

All intellectual property, including patents, trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets, and know-how, developed by TargetCo employees during their employment, whether or not resulting from their employment duties, shall be transferred to AcquirerCorp as part of the asset acquisition.

AcquirerCorp may unilaterally modify the allocation of the purchase price among acquired assets for tax purposes without the consent of TargetCo or its shareholders, and TargetCo agrees to be bound by and file all tax returns consistent with AcquirerCorp's allocation.

The indemnification obligations of TargetCo shareholders under this Agreement shall be joint and several. Each shareholder's liability shall be proportionate to their ownership percentage, except where AcquirerCorp elects to pursue claims jointly.

TargetCo shall conduct its business in the ordinary course between the signing of this Agreement and Closing. Material deviations from ordinary course operations require AcquirerCorp's prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.

If any litigation is pending or threatened against TargetCo as of the Closing Date, AcquirerCorp shall have the right, but not the obligation, to assume control of such litigation at TargetCo's expense. Settlement of any such litigation shall require AcquirerCorp's prior written consent.

Any arbitration proceedings commenced pursuant to this Agreement shall be conducted in English in New York, New York under JAMS International Rules. The arbitration panel shall consist of one arbitrator selected by mutual agreement. The arbitrator's decision shall be final and binding.

AcquirerCorp may assign this Agreement or any of its rights hereunder to any affiliate or successor entity without the consent of TargetCo. TargetCo may not assign any rights or obligations without AcquirerCorp's prior written consent.

In the event AcquirerCorp terminates this Agreement for any reason other than a material breach by TargetCo, AcquirerCorp shall pay a break-up fee of Two Million Dollars ($2,000,000) to TargetCo as liquidated damages.

TargetCo warrants that its software products do not infringe upon the intellectual property rights of any third party. In the event of any IP infringement claim post-closing, TargetCo shareholders shall indemnify AcquirerCorp for all related damages, costs, and expenses without any cap or time limitation.

The parties agree that the allocation of risk and limitation of liability provisions set forth in this Agreement represent a fair and reasonable allocation of risk between sophisticated commercial parties negotiating at arm's length.

AcquirerCorp's obligation to close the transaction is conditioned upon the satisfaction, in AcquirerCorp's sole and absolute discretion, of all conditions precedent, including receipt of financing, regulatory approvals, and satisfactory due diligence results.

TargetCo's existing agreements with key customers, suppliers, and partners may contain change-of-control provisions that could result in termination or renegotiation. The impact of these provisions is not fully known at the time of execution of this term sheet.

TargetCo shareholders who are also employees agree to enter into new employment agreements with AcquirerCorp post-closing on terms to be negotiated in good faith. Failure to reach agreement on employment terms shall not constitute grounds to terminate this Agreement.

AcquirerCorp shall have the right to conduct full forensic audits of TargetCo's financial records, IT systems, and operations for a period of three years post-closing, and TargetCo and its former principals shall provide reasonable cooperation.

Representations and warranties regarding TargetCo's compliance with data protection laws, including GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy regulations, shall survive the Closing indefinitely and are not subject to the survival period limitation set forth in Clause 9.

In the event of any conflict between this term sheet and any subsequent definitive agreement, the terms of this term sheet shall prevail unless the definitive agreement expressly references and supersedes specific provisions herein.

AcquirerCorp may, without additional consideration, require TargetCo to provide transitional services for a period of up to 24 months post-closing, including IT support, accounting, legal, and HR services. The scope and duration of such services shall be determined by AcquirerCorp at its sole discretion.

The governing law clause conflicts with the arbitration venue clause in ways that may create legal uncertainty regarding which jurisdiction's procedural rules and substantive law apply in the event of a dispute.

This term sheet is binding upon execution and shall be enforceable to the same extent as a definitive agreement. The parties acknowledge that this term sheet contains all material terms and that execution of a more formal document is not a condition to any party's obligations hereunder.
